2603.20161.title: Semantic Token Clustering for Efficient Uncertainty Quantification in Large Language Models
2603.20161.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse tasks. However, the truthfulness of their outputs is not guaranteed, and their tendency toward overconfidence further limits reliability. Uncertainty quantification offers a promising way to identify potentially unreliable outputs, but most existing methods rely on repeated sampling or auxiliary models, introducing substantial computational overhead. To address these limitations, we propose Semantic Token Clustering (STC), an efficient uncertainty quantification method that leverages the semantic information inherently encoded in LLMs. Specifically, we group tokens into semantically consistent clusters using embedding clustering and prefix matching, and quantify uncertainty based on the probability mass aggregated over the corresponding semantic cluster. Our approach requires only a single generation and does not depend on auxiliary models. Experimental results show that STC achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art baselines while substantially reducing computational overhead.
2603.20161.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在各種任務中展現了卓越的能力。<lb/>然而，它們輸出的真實性並不保證，且過度自信的傾向進一步限制了可靠性。<lb/>不確定性量化提供了一種有前景的方法來識別潛在的不可靠輸出，但大多數現有方法依賴於重複取樣或輔助模型，這引入了相當大的計算開銷。<lb/>為了解決這些限制，我們提出了語義標記聚類（STC），這是一種高效的不確定性量化方法，利用LLMs中固有編碼的語義信息。<lb/>具體而言，我們使用嵌入聚類和前綴匹配將標記分組成語義一致的聚類，並根據相應語義聚類上聚合的概率質量來量化不確定性。<lb/>我們的方法僅需一次生成，且不依賴於輔助模型。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，STC在性能上與最先進的基準相當，同時顯著降低了計算開銷。
2603.20161.author: Qi Cao et.al.
2603.20161.authors: Qi Cao, Andrew Gambardella, Takeshi Kojima, Yutaka Matsuo, Yusuke Iwasawa
